'''''Star Wars''''' was a group of comic book series, specials and annuals published by [[Marvel Comics]]' United Kingdom imprint.<ref name="SW.com Weekly">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-in-the-uk-star-wars-weekly|text=''Star Wars'' in the UK: ''Star Wars Weekly''}}</ref>

==Development==
The comics began with reprints of Marvel's US [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' (1977)]] series and ''[[Pizzazz]]'' magazine comic stories released under the ''[[Star Wars Weekly]]'' title, starting on [[February 8]], [[1978]].<ref name="SWW1" /> The main series went through several name changes throughout the years before its end on [[June 7]], [[1986]].<ref name="ROTJW155">[[Return of the Jedi Weekly 155|''Return of the Jedi Weekly'' 155]]</ref> All titles continued a single numbering system until ''[[Return of the Jedi Weekly]]'', which reset the numbering to #1.<ref name="ROTJW1">[[Return of the Jedi Weekly 1|''Return of the Jedi Weekly'' 1]]</ref>

The main difference between the British series and its American counterpart was that the former followed the then-common tradition of weekly British comics of being an anthology title, with several serialised stories each issue. It would take at least two weekly issues to publish all the content printed in only one issue of the US original. Also, unlike the US title, the interior of the British weekly was monochrome, on matte paper, with only the front and back covers featuring full colour printing, on glossy paper. More than one story would often run concurrently. Many issues also included articles and interviews, as well as additional stories from other Marvel Comics series not directly related to ''Star Wars''.<ref name="SW.com Weekly" />

This series did eventually contain original stories that did not appear in either the US ''Star Wars'' series or ''Pizzazz''. Reprints of these stories in the US would happen over many years, the first being two Marvel Illustrated Books trade paperback collections titled [[Star Wars (Marvel Illustrated Books)|''Star Wars'']] and ''[[Star Wars 2: World of Fire]]'' in [[1981]] and [[1982]]. No other UK-exclusive stories were reprinted until [[Dark Horse Comics]]' ''[[Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds]]'' [[1996]] miniseries.<ref name="Devilworlds1">[[Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds 1|''Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds'' 1]]</ref><ref name="Devilworlds2">[[Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds 2|''Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds'' 2]]</ref> The one remaining story never published after that, "[[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149|Death Masque]]," was finally reprinted in [[2013]] along with the other UK stories in ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 1]]''.<ref name="Wild Space 1">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 1]]''</ref>

After the regular series ended, Marvel UK continued republishing stories from the US [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Star Wars: Ewoks'' (1985)]] and ''[[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|Droids]]'' comics up until [[1989]]. The last original stories were short text stories printed in ''[[Ewoks Annual]]'', which were not reprinted until [[2016]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Droids & Ewoks Omnibus]]'' from Marvel,<ref name="D&E Omnibus">''[[Star Wars: Droids & Ewoks Omnibus]]''</ref> followed shortly by [[2017]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Marvel UK Collection Omnibus]]''.<ref name="Marvel UK Collection">''[[Star Wars: The Marvel UK Collection Omnibus]]''</ref>

====Anthology series====
These series included comic stories from various licensed comics, including reprints from US ''Star Wars'' comics:
*''Spidey Comic'' 661–666: [[Ewoks (1985) 2|''Ewoks'' (1985) 2]] and [[Ewoks (1985) 3|''Ewoks'' (1985) 3]] {{C|[[November 9]]-[[December 14]], [[1985]]}}
*''Spider-Man and Zoids'' 15–22: [[Star Wars Annual (1977) 3|''Star Wars Annual'' 3]] {{C|[[June 14]]-[[August 12]], [[1986]]}}
*''[[The Marvel Bumper Comic]]'' 14–25: Stories from the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] comic book
